investments-in-2016.notes: Hint: use CTE, concat subquery for location, use exclusive logic using count and group

consecutive-numbers.notes: Hint: CTE full join using union

department-top-three-salaries.notes: Hint: CTE + inner join + dense_rank with partition to rank

tree-node.notes: Hint: use case and subquery to see if a node already exists as a p_id

exchange-seats.notes: Hint: use case and modulo to change the IDs, then reorder the IDs, don't forget about the last odd seat

customers-who-bought-all-products.notes: Hint: group by id, then use having and subquery counter

product-sales-analysis-iii.notes: Hint: subquery to store the true values of min when a tie occurs

product-price-at-a-given-date.notes: Hint: subquery to find the most recent pair of price and product ID, use union to make a default of 10 for unchanged prices before the date

market-analysis-i.notes: Hint: use left join to keep people with 0 orders; filters should be after the on clause for easy

last-person-to-fit-in-the-bus.notes: Hint: CTE to track the running total, use order by and limit in the main query to retrieve the last person

immediate-food-delivery-ii.notes: Hint: case to add to immediate orders, subquery to filter for first orders, don't use CTE

trips-and-users.notes: Hint: CTE and double inner join to filter banned users, case to sum cancels, and group by date

monthly-transactions-i.notes: Hint: use date_format to get rid of the day portion of the date; repeat when multigrouping

queries-quality-and-percentage.notes: Hint: pure math, don't forget to round

restaurant-growth.notes: Hint: use a CTE to count the number of customers in the given time frame and find the daily sum of all customers for every day, main query to find moving sum and moving average, use offset to remove days with invalid windows
